title: rosa rubiginosa subvar. purpurea
intro: Rosa rubiginosa subvar. purpurea, also known as the sweetbriar or fragrant sweetbriar, is a type of flowering shrub native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its clusters of small, fragrant, and deep purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Rosa rubiginosa subvar. purpurea include reducing inflammation, treating wounds and skin conditions, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues such as colds and coughs. The herbal preparations of this plant typically include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
